_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron)
_meta.note: Palm Bay, Florida — complete sitting council cohort (5/5): Mayor Medina (Seat — Mayor at-large), Deputy Mayor Jaffe (Seat 5), Council Hammer (Seat 2), Council Langevin (Seat 3), Council Johnson (Seat 4). Rosters web-verified June 2026 against palmbayfl.gov, Ballotpedia, and The Palm Bayer election-results coverage. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a primary or reputable URL; null where no evidence (no party-default inference, no biography-to-policy inference). HIGH BARS HONORED: public_justice.0 requires affirmative police-advocacy beyond a budget vote (campaign program / on-record statement / endorsement-acceptance with platform); refuse_state_overreach.0 requires resistance to an UNCONSTITUTIONAL state mandate (compliance with FL Surgeon-General fluoride guidance or with state-directed 287(g) does NOT score here). SLUG COLLISIONS DEFERRED THIS BATCH: rick-west (VA Chesapeake Mayor IDX 497 + OK State Rep IDX 5439 — by_slug winner is the OK record, so refining the VA Mayor without a slug rename would clobber the OK rep) — substituted Mayor Medina to complete the Palm Bay cohort. PALM BAY GROUP CONTEXT: 287(g) ICE MOA April 3 2025 (5-0 unanimous); fluoride removal Jan 2 2025 (5-0); Sept 8 2025 millage 4-1 (Langevin alone NAY for HIGHER rate); Nov 2024 CM Sherman firing 3-2 (Medina + Jaffe NO); April 2025 CM Morton appointment 4-1; Oct 17 2025 Langevin censure 3-2 (Hammer + Langevin NO, Jaffe authored motion, Medina supported). NONPARTISAN OFFICE: party field updated only with documented self-identification or registration evidence (Langevin = R confirmed; Johnson = D-aligned per BrevardDems mobilize-events; Hammer / Jaffe / Medina = no Republican primary registration confirmed but all are documented R-coded by endorsements + behavior — left party NULL absent primary-source confirmation). LANGEVIN CENSURE CANDOR: dossier explicitly records the Oct 17 2025 censure + federal lawsuit + anti-Indian-American + Confederate-soldier-praise + Robert-Rundo-pardon-letter facts in notes_set per the brand's 'honesty over salesmanship' voice; cells are scored on the rubric questions, not on civic-conduct findings the rubric doesn't cover.

set.office: Mayor of Palm Bay, Florida (first elected Nov 2020 · re-elected outright Aug 20, 2024 with 55.33% over Rosado / Rivera / Wright · sworn in Nov 20, 2024 · 4-yr term to Nov 2028 · next mayoral election Nov 2028)

profile.religion: Christian — explicitly professed; MA Theology magna cum laude from Life Christian University; Director of Ministry at The House Family Ministries Church in Palm Bay. On record: 'I serve my Lord and Savior. First and foremost, I represent him, and he's guiding my steps' (Fox35 Orlando, April 2023).
profile.background: Rob Medina, b. 1961 in New York; ~38-year Palm Bay resident. U.S. Marine Corps veteran (Reagan era). 16 years as Director of Community and Military Relations to Rep. Bill Posey (R, FL-8) and Rep. Mike Haridopolos (R, FL-15); earlier legislative assistant to Brevard Commissioner Andy Anderson and Commissioner Trudie Infantini. MA Theology, Life Christian University. Director of Ministry, The House Family Ministries Church. Marine Corps League Det. 918. Married to Lissette, 6 children, 10 grandchildren. First elected Mayor 2020; re-elected outright Aug 2024 (no runoff needed). Nonpartisan office; documented Republican alignment via Rubio / Haridopolos / Crisafulli / Sheriff Ivey / SA Phil Archer endorsements.

rob-medina.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Mayor of Palm Bay as of June 2026 (palmbayfl.gov bio + ongoing Mayor's Messages through June 2026 + 2024 re-election certification). Sweep of palmbayfl.gov, robmedinaforpbmayor.com, Space Coast Daily, The Space Coast Rocket, The Palm Bayer, FL Voice News, Fox35 Orlando, ClickOrlando, Spectrum News 13, CFPublic, Florida Mayors Assn, Ballotpedia, his Facebook + X + LinkedIn. HARD evidence — 5 TRUE / 0 FALSE / 25 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: christian_liberty.0 (open Christian profession 'I serve my Lord and Savior ... There is no separation of church and state'); christian_liberty.3 (Dec 2024 + Dec 2025 Mayor's Messages signed 'HAPPY BIRTHDAY, JESUS!' + National Day of Prayer in Council Chambers + campaign tagline 'Servant Leader through God, Honor & Integrity'); public_justice.0 (FOP Lodge #111 + Palm Bay Firefighters Local 2446 + Sheriff Ivey + SA Phil Archer endorsements; participated in PBPD Citizens Academy; 2025 State of City highlighted return of General Crimes Unit; March 2026 message emphasized officer mental health — affirmative advocacy chain beyond budget); sanctity_of_life.4 (Jan 2026 Mayor's Message + April 18 2026 Mayor's Ball elevated Essential Pregnancy Center as beneficiary — affirmative pro-life-center elevation; no PP/NARAL/EMILY's List record located); border_immigration.2 (Aye on Palm Bay 287(g) ICE deputization, 5-0, April 3, 2025 — anti-sanctuary cooperation). NULL HOLDS (downgraded from possible agent calls per skill discipline): biblical_marriage q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 ALL NULL — despite his theology degree + ministry role, NO citable Pride proclamation, marriage statement, or LGBTQ-ideology statement located; rubric requires affirmative statement/vote, not biography. family_child_sovereignty ALL NULL — out of city-mayor jurisdiction and no statements. economic_stewardship.2 NULL (not FALSE): Medina was the HIGH-rate advocate on Sept 8 2025 millage (pushed 6.7339 staff figure, ended at 6.7000 — 4-1) — not a restraint posture, but not affirmatively pro-deficit either; hostile-reader flag noted. refuse_state_overreach ALL NULL — no resistance-to-unconstitutional-state-mandate record; the fluoride-removal vote (Jan 2 2025, 5-0, Langevin moved) was COMPLIANCE with FL Surgeon-General Ladapo's Nov 2024 guidance, not refusal. self_defense ALL NULL — Sheriff Ivey called him 'a patriot and advocate of our constitutional rights' but vibes are not a citable cell-position. election_integrity ALL NULL. ISRAEL FLAG NOTE: lost 1-4 bid to fly Israel flag at City Hall — sustains christian_liberty disposition but adds no new TRUE cells. CENSURE / FIRING NOTES (not scored): Medina voted NO on the Nov 2024 firing of CM Sherman (3-2 lost) and supported the Oct 17 2025 censure of Councilman Langevin (3-2) — internal-conduct matters, not rubric cells.

0.note: Direct quote (April 2023 Fox35 Orlando): 'I serve my Lord and Savior. First and foremost, I represent him, and he's guiding my steps' + 'There is no separation of church and state. The divine that's inside of you is who leads you in making decisions' — affirmative public profession of Christ in the Mayor's office

3.note: Dec 2024 + Dec 2025 official Mayor's Messages signed 'HAPPY BIRTHDAY, JESUS!' and refer to 'the presence of our Savior'; campaign tagline 'A Servant Leader through God, Honor & Integrity'; hosts National Day of Prayer event in Palm Bay Council Chambers — affirmative public-square Christian symbolism in his official capacity

0.note: Endorsed by Palm Bay FOP Lodge #111, Palm Bay Professional Firefighters Local 2446, Palm Bay Police Retirees Group, Sheriff Wayne Ivey, State Attorney Phil Archer; personally completed PBPD Citizens Academy (April 2026 message); 2025 State of City highlighted 'return of the Police Department's General Crimes Unit'; March 2026 message emphasized 'officer mental health' — sustained affirmative pro-police advocacy beyond budget

2.note: April 3, 2025 — Aye on unanimous 5-0 Palm Bay 287(g) Task Force Model MOA with ICE/DHS deputizing local police for federal immigration enforcement (40-hr DHS training); affirmative anti-sanctuary / pro-federal-preemption posture

set.office: Deputy Mayor / City Council Seat 5, City of Palm Bay, Florida (elected Nov 5, 2024 with 44.24% in 5-way special; sworn in + unanimously elevated to Deputy Mayor Nov 20, 2024; Seat 5 on Nov 3, 2026 regular ballot — re-election filed July 1, 2025)

profile.background: Mike Jaffe — 20+ year Palm Bay resident. Director of Operations for a regional home builder; small-business owner; licensed FL realtor. Married 22 years to high-school sweetheart Kim; two children (ages 9 and 7). Little League coach. Self-described fiscal conservative focused on infrastructure (flooding / Indian River Lagoon), public safety budget protection, and responsible development. Reports knocking 5,000+ doors during 2024 campaign. Won Seat 5 with 44.24% on Nov 5, 2024 (5-way special election); sworn in + unanimously elevated to Deputy Mayor Nov 20, 2024 (Hammer nominated). Authored the Oct 17, 2025 motion to censure colleague Chandler Langevin (3-2) over Langevin's 'Deport every Indian immediately' remarks and citation of Robert Rundo, requesting Gov. DeSantis suspend Langevin under FS 112.51 + an ethics complaint. Soft-R signal: spoke at the Brevard Republican Party Straw Poll (June 22, 2024); no primary-source party registration confirmed. Announced re-election bid July 1, 2025.

mike-jaffe.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Deputy Mayor + Seat 5 Councilman of Palm Bay as of June 2026 (palmbayfl.gov bio + Nov 2024 election certification + The Palm Bayer swearing-in coverage). Sweep of palmbayfl.gov, jaffeforpalmbay.com, The Palm Bayer, The Space Coast Rocket, Space Coast Daily, Hometown News Brevard, FL Voice News, CFPublic, Spectrum News 13, his Facebook campaign page. HARD evidence — 3 TRUE / 0 FALSE / 27 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: economic_stewardship.2 (campaign platform commits to 1-mill property-tax reduction in FY 2026-27 via 20% across-the-board budget cuts; mandated 'Owner Direct Purchasing' to eliminate contractor markups; saved ~$50K on city-manager search by foregoing outside consultants — affirmative restraint posture beyond routine governance, despite mixed-signal Sept 8 2025 vote AYE on 6.7000 millage and his characterization of below-rate advocates as engaged in 'political play'); public_justice.0 (campaign program funds $3.7M for two new temporary fire stations + permanent buildouts of Stations 8 and 9; procurement of 24 replacement patrol vehicles; places police HQ + substations 'in high-growth corridors' as policy benchmark; ties development approvals to public-safety response times — affirmative pro-LE program beyond budget); border_immigration.2 (Aye on unanimous 5-0 Palm Bay 287(g) MOA April 3, 2025). NULL HOLDS (downgraded per skill): religion NULL — no faith profession found; don't infer from Jewish-sounding surname. Public_justice.1 NULL on Langevin-censure motion — that targets a colleague's racist speech under FS 112 ethics rather than the rubric's rogue-prosecutor/cashless-bail cell; ethics-enforcement-on-colleague is adjacent but not the same cell; honest discipline kept NULL. All other 26 cells NULL after exhaustive sweep — no public statements on life, marriage, family/schools, religious-liberty, election integrity beyond vague 'Integrity should always be first when representing the citizens of Palm Bay,' refuse-state-overreach (was not in office during COVID), broader border cells, or 2A.

2.note: Campaign platform commits to a 1-mill property-tax reduction in FY 2026-27 via 20% across-the-board departmental budget cuts; mandated 'Owner Direct Purchasing' to eliminate contractor markups; saved ~$50K on city-manager search by foregoing outside consultants — affirmative restraint posture beyond routine governance. Mixed signal noted: Sept 8 2025 voted AYE on 6.7000 millage (4-1; Langevin alone NAY for higher rate) and characterized below-rate advocates as engaged in 'political play' that would 'set up the city manager to fail' — held net-TRUE on platform-level + Feb 2026 cuts push; hostile-reader flag in audit trail

0.note: Campaign program explicitly funds $3.7M for two new temporary fire stations + permanent buildouts of Stations 8 and 9, procurement of 24 replacement police patrol vehicles, places police HQ + substations 'in high-growth corridors' as a policy benchmark, and ties development approvals to public-safety response times; Sept 2025 millage rate explicitly framed around 'police and fire department solvency' — affirmative pro-LE program beyond routine budget vote

2.note: Aye on unanimous 5-0 vote April 3, 2025 to enroll Palm Bay PD in ICE's 287(g) Task Force Model after 40-hr DHS training — affirmative anti-sanctuary / pro-federal-preemption posture

set.office: City Council Seat 2, City of Palm Bay, Florida (elected Nov 5, 2024 with 52.99% over incumbent Donny Felix; sworn in Nov 20, 2024 · 4-yr term to Nov 2028 · Seat 2 not on 2026 ballot)

profile.background: Richard 'Mike' Hammer — Palm Bay native, descendant of the founding Platt family; 22 years coaching youth football. Professional background in criminal justice and emergency medical response with civil/criminal law expertise; fire-science coursework at Indian River State College. Ran as a fiscal-restraint / 'smart growth' challenger funded by individual donors (refused developer money). Represents Palm Bay on the Brevard County Transportation Planning Organization. Voted AGAINST the Oct 17, 2025 censure of Councilman Chandler Langevin on First Amendment grounds while explicitly disagreeing with Langevin's anti-Indian-American remarks. Nonpartisan office; no primary-source party registration confirmed in this sweep.

mike-hammer.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Seat 2 Councilman of Palm Bay as of June 2026 (palmbayfl.gov bio + Ballotpedia 2024 race + The Palm Bayer certification coverage). Sweep of palmbayfl.gov, voteforhammer.com, his Facebook + LinkedIn, Hometown News Brevard, The Palm Bayer, The Space Coast Rocket, Spectrum News 13, CFPublic, FL Voice News, ClickOrlando. HARD evidence — 3 TRUE / 0 FALSE / 27 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: economic_stewardship.2 (Sept 8 2025 budget hearing — Hammer + Johnson advocated for the LOWER millage; rate dropped from staff-proposed 6.7339 to 6.7000 with Hammer Aye on the lower figure; campaigned on operational audit + ending 'excessive administrative bloat, wasteful spending' and 'reliance on costly consultants'; refused developer donations — affirmative restraint posture beyond a routine vote); public_justice.0 (campaign platform explicitly proposed 'adding 40 police officers and an auxiliary station in the Compound' funded by impact fees; supported and Aye on the Feb 5 2026 police staffing study funded by impact fees; personal background in criminal justice; Feb 2026 statement on fire-hydrant levels-of-service as 'one of the most important things in our toolbox' — affirmative pro-LE program); border_immigration.2 (Aye on unanimous 5-0 Palm Bay 287(g) ICE MOA April 3, 2025). NOT-SCORED (NULL held strict): Jan 2 2025 fluoride-removal vote (5-0; Langevin moved) — vote ALIGNED with FL Surgeon-General Ladapo's Nov 2024 guidance against community water fluoridation; that is COMPLIANCE with state guidance, not REFUSAL of an unconstitutional state mandate; refuse_state_overreach.0 NULL. Oct 17 2025 Langevin-censure NO vote on First Amendment grounds is a free-speech / Bill-of-Rights-defense act for content-neutral protection of a colleague's offensive speech; christian_liberty cells require Christ-profession or compelled-speech-against-Christian-conviction — censure of anti-Indian-American + Confederate-praise speech does not map cleanly to any christian_liberty cell; per skill discipline kept NULL rather than force-fit. All other 26 cells NULL after exhaustive sweep — no statements on life, marriage, family/schools, religious liberty, election integrity, broader public-justice cells, broader refuse-state-overreach, broader border, or 2A.

2.note: Sept 8 2025 budget hearing — Hammer + Johnson advocated for a lower millage; rate dropped from staff-proposed 6.7339 to 6.7000 with Hammer voting Aye on the lower figure; campaigned on operational audit + ending 'excessive administrative bloat, wasteful spending' and 'reliance on costly consultants'; refused developer donations — affirmative fiscal-restraint posture beyond a routine vote

0.note: Campaign platform explicitly proposed 'adding 40 police officers and an auxiliary station in the Compound' funded by impact fees; supported and Aye on the Feb 5 2026 police staffing study funded by impact fees; personal background in criminal justice — affirmative pro-LE program beyond routine budget vote

2.note: Aye on unanimous 5-0 vote April 3, 2025 to enroll Palm Bay PD in ICE's 287(g) Task Force Model after 40-hr DHS training — affirmative anti-sanctuary / pro-federal-preemption posture

set.office: City Council Seat 3, City of Palm Bay, Florida (elected Nov 5, 2024 with 50.58% over Alfy Agarie · ~639-vote margin · sworn in Nov 20, 2024 · 4-yr term to Nov 2028 · Seat 3 not on 2026 ballot · CENSURED Oct 17, 2025 by 3-2 vote)

profile.religion: Christian — explicitly professed: 'I will choose God over humanism, our children and parents over indoctrination, and our brave law enforcement officers' (STARRS interview). Denomination not specified.
profile.background: Chandler Langevin — Brevard County native, Titusville High grad. ~10 years U.S. Navy Hospital Corpsman (FMF) attached to USMC — NATO Liaison with 4th ANGLICO in Afghanistan; sniper-platoon medic with 1st Bn 25th Marines in the Pacific. Took early honorable discharge over the Biden COVID vaccine mandate ('unwillingness to be a guinea pig'). Works in veteran advocacy; founded local youth program 'Florida Scouts'; State Director, American Restoration Coalition. Previously on Odyssey Charter School board (removed Oct 2025 over the censure controversy). Briefly filed for FL HD-33 in 2023 before withdrawing and endorsing Monique Miller. Active with Republican Liberty Caucus (Brevard chapter), Moms for Liberty (Brevard), and Space Coast Young Republicans. CENSURE / LITIGATION FACTS (recorded candidly per brand voice): On Oct 17, 2025 Palm Bay Council voted 3-2 to censure Langevin and ask Gov. DeSantis to suspend him under FS 112.51 after Langevin posted on X 'Deport every Indian immediately,' Confederate-soldier-praise posts including a description of Robert E. Lee as 'a Godly and honorable presence,' and a request on city letterhead asking President Trump to pardon white-nationalist activist Robert Rundo (founder of the Rise Above Movement). Langevin filed a federal First Amendment suit (Langevin v. Palm Bay) represented by Anthony Sabatini; bench trial began Jan 21, 2026.

chandler-langevin.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Seat 3 Councilman of Palm Bay as of June 2026 (palmbayfl.gov bio + Ballotpedia 2024 race + The Palm Bayer + multiple Oct 2025 censure / lawsuit reports). Sweep of palmbayfl.gov, chandlerlangevin.com, his Facebook + X (@ChandlerForPB), STARRS, FL Voice News, Space Coast Daily, The Space Coast Rocket, Hometown News Brevard, WFTV, ClickOrlando, Spectrum News 13 (mynews13), CFPublic, The Palm Bayer, RLC FL endorsement page, Florida Politics. PARTY CORRECTION applied (R — confirmed: self-identified 'Palm Bay Republican', ran R for FL HD-33 in 2023, RLC endorsee, Moms for Liberty + Space Coast Young Republicans active). HARD evidence — 6 TRUE / 1 FALSE / 23 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: christian_liberty.0 ('I will choose God over humanism' STARRS framing of public role as Christian-citizenship); family_child_sovereignty.1 (affirmative parental-rights framing 'our children and parents over indoctrination' + Odyssey Charter School board + founded Florida Scouts youth alternative + Moms for Liberty Brevard affiliation); public_justice.0 (campaign 'law and order' frame — 'Chandler will work with Palm Bay Police and Fire to ensure they are properly staffed, paid, highly equipped'; 90-day report praised PBPD as 'highest caliber I have seen' with commitment to expand presence; STARRS interview elevated 'brave law enforcement officers' as a core priority — affirmative advocacy beyond budget); refuse_state_overreach.2 (resigned from active military rather than comply with Biden COVID vaccination mandate; led successful Jan 2, 2025 5-0 city vote to permanently end Palm Bay fluoridation on bodily-autonomy grounds — 'We are force-medicating our entire population if we do this, and that's just not right. It's a personal choice... with any other medical decision, the government doesn't make that for you' — affirmative protection of citizens from health-mandate overreach); border_immigration.1 (repeated public posture demanding mandatory deportation: 'Deport every Indian immediately'; 'My message to end all illegal immigration and most legal immigration is critical to my constituents'; used city letterhead to advocate stricter immigration enforcement); border_immigration.2 (publicly opposes any sanctuary stance; calls for revoking H-1B visas and 'most legal immigration'; aligned with federal preemption); self_defense.0 (organized Sept 20 2025 Charlie Kirk memorial march at which he and others openly carried firearms — affirmative public exercise/normalization of FL constitutional-carry framework; RLC endorsement). FALSE — R-DEVIATION FLAG: economic_stewardship.2 (Sept 8 2025 millage vote — Langevin voted NAY on the LOWER 6.7000 rate because he wanted the HIGHER 6.7339 rate; the lone vote against the rate-cut, said he was 'not here to budge' or 'negotiate' — directionally opposite of fiscal-restraint cell as written, despite his stated 'keeping taxes down' philosophy; cited vote outweighs rhetoric per skill discipline). NULL HOLDS: sanctity_of_life ALL NULL — $300 donation from 'Faith Family Freedom Coalition' is donor inference not his statement. biblical_marriage ALL NULL — no Pride proclamation vote, drag-restriction vote, or pronoun-policy vote in his Palm Bay term; his broader 'anti-woke' framing is content-light. family_child_sovereignty q0/q2 NULL — no specific K-12 SOGI/CRT vote at council level. christian_liberty q3 NULL — no documented invocation-prayer defense vote. economic_stewardship q0/q1/q3 NULL. election_integrity ALL NULL. public_justice q1/q3/q4 NULL. refuse_state_overreach q0/q3/q4 NULL — COVID/fluoride is q2-coded already. border_immigration q0 NULL — no specific 287(g) procedural-author statement located (was sitting on the 5-0 vote but did not move it). self_defense q1 NULL — no specific AWB/mag-limit/red-flag floor vote. SOURCE-GAP NOTE: chandlerlangevin.com + hometownnewsbrevard.com candidate questionnaire pages returned 403/429 during sweep — a manual revisit could surface additional life/marriage/election-integrity cells.

Cited vote outweighs rhetoric per skill discipline

set.office: City Council Seat 4 / Deputy Mayor designate, City of Palm Bay, Florida (first elected 2018; re-elected Nov 2022; current term to Nov 2026; Seat 4 on Nov 3, 2026 regular ballot — re-election bid filed via johnson4palmbay.com)

profile.religion: Christian — publicly self-identified: 'committed to aligning with individuals who dedicate their talents and skills to advance God's purpose and fulfill the Great Commission'; mission 'to bring unity within the body of Christ, fostering connections that are grounded in love, service, and faith.' Fellowship of Christian Athletes 2005-2012; has spoken at Greater Palm Bay Church of God events. Denomination not specified.
profile.background: Kenny Johnson, MPA — Melbourne, FL native; Bayside High School; full football scholarship to Charleston Southern University (defensive back); B.S. Criminal Justice; Master of Public Administration. Member of Omega Psi Phi Fraternity. Returned to Brevard 2013; juvenile case manager, juvenile detention officer, teacher, coach, personal trainer. Founded Top Notch Training of Brevard, Inc. (2016) and the 'Top Notch Leaders' mentoring program for 5th-6th-grade boys. First elected to Palm Bay Council Seat 4 in 2018 (~58%); re-elected 2022. Currently Deputy Mayor (rotating peer designation, sequence overlaps with Jaffe) + Chair of the Florida League of Cities Finance & Taxation Policy Committee. Established Palm Bay's Annual Black History Event, Inaugural Veterans Day Parade, Youth Advisory Board, and Civil Citation Program for misdemeanor cannabis (diversion alternative to arrest). Father; local business owner. Notably feuded with then-State Rep Randy Fine (2022 confrontation; State Attorney declined to charge Johnson — 'no prosecutable crimes').

kenny-johnson.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Seat 4 Councilman of Palm Bay as of June 2026 (palmbayfl.gov staff directory + johnson4palmbay.com re-election campaign site + Hometown News Brevard 2021 profile + multiple 2024-2026 news mentions). Sweep of palmbayfl.gov, johnson4palmbay.com, his Facebook + Instagram + LinkedIn, Hometown News Brevard, The Space Coast Rocket, The Palm Bayer, FL Voice News, WFTV, ClickOrlando, Spectrum News 13, CFPublic, mobilize.us / BrevardDems pages, Greater Palm Bay Church of God videos. PARTY NOTE: Palm Bay city offices are officially nonpartisan; Johnson himself has not publicly stated a party label, but his civic activity is openly Democratic-aligned (FL Democratic Party / BrevardDems hosted 'Palm Bay Knockathon with Kenny Johnson' voter-canvass events at precincts 310, 313, 510) — left party field NULL absent primary-source registration confirmation. HARD evidence — 3 TRUE / 0 FALSE / 27 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: economic_stewardship.2 (at Sept 8 2025 budget hearing Johnson + Hammer advocated for a LOWER millage; Johnson on record city must 'trim fat' when 'things get tight for the residents' and pushed 'zero-based budgeting'; voted Aye on the compromise 6.7000 rate — affirmative restraint posture beyond a routine vote, cross-direction for a D-aligned councilor); public_justice.0 (campaign site / city bio documents Johnson 'led efforts in funding our police and fire departments, along with creating new community policing programs that have kept crime rates low and increased trust in law enforcement'; statements at the Compound-crime workshop pushed police-led safety solutions; Aye on $78,500 police staffing study Feb 5 2026; Compound rezoning push tied directly to public-safety justification — affirmative advocacy beyond a budget vote); border_immigration.2 (Aye on unanimous 5-0 Palm Bay 287(g) MOA April 3 2025 — affirmative anti-sanctuary cooperation, cross-direction for a D-aligned councilor). NULL HOLDS (downgraded per skill): christian_liberty ALL NULL — personal Christian profession is biographical (goes in profile.religion), but cell-positions require policy acts/votes, none located. Civil-Citation Program for misdemeanor cannabis is soft-on-low-level-drug enforcement but does not map cleanly to any public_justice cell as written → NULL with note rather than force-fit. refuse_state_overreach.0 NULL — rubric targets resistance to UNCONSTITUTIONAL state mandates; 287(g) Aye is COMPLIANCE with state direction, not resistance. All other 26 cells NULL after exhaustive sweep — no Pride/drag/SOGI vote at Palm Bay during his tenure, no abortion/personhood statement, no election-integrity statement, no 2A statement (FL preempts most local gun regulation), no broader refuse-state-overreach or border-immigration cells. DEVIATION ALERT (D-aligned with R-coded votes): Johnson is openly D-aligned but VOTES consistently fiscally-conservative (pushed below-staff millage, 'trim fat,' zero-based budgeting) and voted YES on 287(g) ICE MOA — exactly the cross-pressure profile the brand should surface honestly.

2.note: Sept 8 2025 budget hearing — Johnson + Hammer advocated for a LOWER millage rate than staff proposed; Johnson on record city must 'trim fat' when 'things get tight for the residents' and pushed 'zero-based budgeting'; voted Aye on the compromise 6.7000 rate (4-1; Langevin alone NAY for higher) — affirmative fiscal-restraint posture, cross-direction for a D-aligned councilor

0.note: Campaign site / city bio documents Johnson 'led efforts in funding our police and fire departments, along with creating new community policing programs that have kept crime rates low and increased trust in law enforcement'; Compound-crime workshop statements pushed police-led safety solutions; Aye on $78,500 police staffing study Feb 5 2026; Compound rezoning push tied directly to public-safety justification — affirmative advocacy beyond a budget vote

2.note: Aye on unanimous 5-0 vote April 3, 2025 to enroll Palm Bay PD in ICE's 287(g) Task Force Model — affirmative anti-sanctuary / pro-federal-immigration-cooperation, cross-direction for a D-aligned councilor
